title: PRML v0.1 — Pre-Registered ML Manifest
description: Formal JSON Schema for the PRML v0.1 specification (https://spec.falsify.dev/v0.1). A manifest binds a pre-registered evaluation claim to a SHA-256 hash over canonicalised bytes. CC BY 4.0.

version.description: Specification version. v0.1 manifests use the literal string 'prml/0.1'.

claim_id.description: UUIDv7 identifier for the claim. Recommended: generate fresh per claim. Pattern allows the v7 form.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp marking when the manifest was authored. Used as the canonical pre-registration timestamp.

metric.description: Name of the metric being evaluated. Free-form string but should follow community conventions (e.g. 'accuracy', 'pass_at_1', 'refusal_rate', 'mean_episode_reward').

comparator.description: Direction of the threshold comparison. Required to make the claim falsifiable.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold against which the observed metric value is compared via 'comparator'.

hash.description: Content-addressable hash of the dataset. SHA-256 hex form for static datasets; opaque identifier (e.g. 'huggingface:revision-d8f3') for platform-anchored datasets; 'n/a-streaming' or 'n/a-deterministic-env' for cases where content hash is undefined.

uri.description: Optional canonical URL for the dataset. Informative; not part of the hash commitment.

dataset.description: Dataset reference. The 'id' is human-readable; the 'hash' is the content commitment.

seed.description: RNG seed used in the evaluation. Accepts uint64 (most common, e.g. NumPy/PyTorch) or int64 (negative seeds in some C/Go runtimes). May be null for non-deterministic systems (live streams, online RLHF).

id.description: Identifier of the producing entity. Domain (e.g. 'falsify.dev'), GitHub handle, ORCID, or any stable identifier.

signature.description: Optional detached signature over the canonical bytes by 'id''s key. Out of scope for v0.1 verifier behaviour; reserved for downstream systems.

id.description: Stable identifier for the model under evaluation (e.g. 'claude-3.5-sonnet@2025-10-01', 'resnet50-fp16').

hash.description: Optional content hash of the model artefact. SHA-256 hex form when present.

uri.description: Optional canonical URL for the model artefact.

model.description: Optional model reference. Bind the manifest to a specific build.

compute_envelope.description: Optional structured description of compute conditions (precision, hardware class, software stack hash). Out of scope for verdict computation in v0.1.

prior_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hex of the manifest this one amends. Forms the audit chain. v0.1 specifies a forward-only chain.

notes.description: Optional free-text rationale. Counts toward the canonical bytes; tampering with notes invalidates the hash.

examples.notes: HumanEval pass@1, n=164 problems, temperature 0.0, single completion per problem.
